open_hooks_to_carry: Tommy comes to Mallick in the kitchen at 6 AM
Tommy tells her about Cliff — the dock, the push, the water — but not about the fire (legal instinct)
Mallick reads him his rights; the other men watch from the great room
Mallick alone in the kitchen — the casserole still in the fridge, untouched
The fantasy football draft board still on the wall with Martin's picks circled in red
The league is over — the final image should be quiet and earned

payoffs_to_land.description: Mallick's full evidentiary map — built from the comedy of corrections across the novel — underpins the case against Tommy. The reader realizes the detective was building the case while appearing to bumble.

prev_tail_excerpt:
...lled like cedar, the cologne Paul had given Martin for his birthday two years ago. The bottle must have been in the drawer. The smell of Martin in the chair where Martin used to sit, in the room where Martin kept his secrets. The safe was behind the lake painting on the far wall, ajar, the way Cliff had left it and they'd left it after, because the safe didn't matter anymore. What it had protected was loose in the world now. What it had contained was sitting on the deck in the cold.

Paul closed his eyes. Through the study window, if he'd looked, he would have seen the deck, and on the deck he would have seen Tommy, still in the chair, still facing the lake, his hands on the armrests of the chair he now owned in the house he now owned on the lake where his best friend had died and his blackmailer had died and his twenty-two years of managed silence were about to end.

But Paul didn't look. He sat in Martin's chair with his eyes closed and his wet socks leaving marks on the hardwood floor and he listened to the loons calling across the water in the dark. The wail this time — the long, rising one, the sound that carried over the lake like grief looking for a place to land. Joel turned a page on his legal pad. The refrigerator clicked off. The house went quiet, the way the house had been quiet at one-thirty on Saturday morning when Martin's heart stopped, the way it would be quiet tomorrow at six when Tommy walked down the stairs and sat across from Detective Mallick in the kitchen and said whatever he was going to say.

Paul listened to the quiet. He listened for a long time.
secondary_character_beats: The surviving men watching Tommy leave should each register differently: Joel the lawyer, Paul the best friend, Nate the narrator, Doug the witness
Mallick's final moment alone should carry the weight of someone who solves crimes for a living and is still affected by this one
state_deltas_required: Tommy's decision and Mallick's final assembly of the case
the mystery resolves — Martin died of natural causes, Cliff was murdered to protect a secret Martin's death set loose, and the league is over
